deck.author: OpenAI Codex
deck.subject: Browser studio for structured presentation authoring
deck.title: slideotter
deck.objective: Introduce new users to slideotter by showing how to choose a presentation, set context, edit structured slides, attach materials, review generated options, validate output, and archive the intended PDF.
deck.constraints: Keep slide copy concise, vary layouts across the tour, and explain the active project without filler or implementation-history detours.
deck.themeBrief: Clean local-workbench presentation with friendly onboarding language, varied information layouts, and restrained technical detail.
deck.outline:
1. slideotter
2. What you will learn
3. Why decks get messy
4. A simple authoring loop
5. Choose a presentation first
6. Give the deck a brief
7. Slides are readable JSON
8. Edit with the slide in view
9. Attach images as materials
10. One renderer keeps previews honest
11. Generate options, not surprises
12. Compare before applying
13. Plan the deck above slide level
14. Use chat for bounded tasks
15. Know what can change
16. Run checks before sharing
17. Archive the version you mean to share
18. How to read the project
19. Good next improvements
20. The basic habit
